item.title: AWS listSshKeys() returns empty publicKey — silent data loss in SshKeyInfo interface
item.intent: Every call to listSshKeys() returns structurally honest data — callers that check publicKey will not silently receive an empty string and mishandle it.
item.description: aws.ts line 301 returns publicKey: '' because AWS EC2 API does not expose public key material after initial import, only fingerprints. The SshKeyInfo interface (types.ts:214-220) defines publicKey: string with no optional marker, so callers expect a real value. Steps: (1) Read src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts lines 292-310 (listSshKeys implementation). (2) Read src/cloud/provisioning/types.ts lines 214-220 (SshKeyInfo interface). (3) Make publicKey optional in SshKeyInfo interface (publicKey?: string). (4) Update the AWS adapter to omit publicKey or set it to undefined rather than empty string. (5) Check all callers of listSshKeys() for publicKey usage — grep for .publicKey in provisioning/. (6) Update any callers that assume publicKey is always truthy to handle undefined. (7) Run cloud/provisioning tests.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts lines 292-310 to confirm listSshKeys() returns publicKey: ''
Read src/cloud/provisioning/types.ts lines 214-220 to confirm SshKeyInfo.publicKey type and optionality
Grep for .publicKey in src/cloud/provisioning/ to find all callers that read the field
Update SshKeyInfo interface: change publicKey: string to publicKey?: string
Update aws.ts listSshKeys(): omit publicKey field or set to undefined rather than empty string
Update any caller that assumes publicKey is always truthy to handle undefined
Run existing cloud/provisioning tests — confirm no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: SshKeyInfo.publicKey is typed as optional (publicKey?: string) in types.ts
AWS listSshKeys() no longer returns publicKey: '' — field is omitted or undefined
DigitalOcean, GCP, Hetzner listSshKeys() implementations unchanged and return real key values
No callers of listSshKeys() assume publicKey is always a non-empty string without a guard
